title: First Minister wants meeting over US military at Prestwick

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: FIRST Minister John Swinney is seeking a meeting with the UK Government over the use of Prestwick Airport by the US military. Earlier this month, Scottish Greens MSP Ross Greer called on Swinney to ban the Americans from using the publicly-owned airport. Critics say that American military planes are stopping off at the South Ayrshire facility on their way to support the US-Israeli offensive against Iran.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: A government spokesperson revealed the First Minister had asked for clarity that US planes were not part of offensive operations, and he also wanted a meeting with the cabinet office to discuss the matter. Speaking on the BBC's Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg programme, Swinney said his government would "explore" and "consider" a ban if it was confirmed the planes were involved in the airstrikes.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: US aircraft are a regular sight at Prestwick Airport, but last month three Boeing KC-135 Stratotankers stopped off, which are not common visitors. Since then, they have flown missions as part of Operation Epic Fury, the name for the US-Israeli war with Iran. First Minister wants meeting over US military at Prestwick | Ayr Advertiser

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – First Minister looks for meeting over US military use of Prestwick Airport
